name: Tyjae Spears
headline: Titans passing-down back carved into a PPR floor
body:
Spears has settled into a clear receiving-back identity. In 2025 he played 13 games and set career highs in catches (45 on 50 targets) and routes (261), turning that into 264 receiving yards and 361 yards after catch, while his rushing slipped to 72 carries for 283 yards at 3.93 YPC. The 45.9 percent snap share and 9.4 percent target share gave him 66.7 fantasy points (RB50), trailing his 2023 rookie peak of 101.8 points (RB38).

The efficiency dip is real: his yards after contact per attempt fell to 1.35 from 2.19 in 2024, and broken tackles cratered from 13 to 4. With the early-down rushing role limited and only 2 rushing scores, his value is tied almost entirely to PPR passing-down volume. That makes him a stable PPR flex with weekly RB2 upside if injuries open the backfield, but a touchdown-dependent, low-ceiling option in standard formats.
